parent.label: Forums & Q&A
parent.description: Informal discussion and community knowledge — the internet arguing, explaining, commiserating, and helping. Reddit, StackExchange, Hacker News. The tone ranges from thoughtful explanation to flame war, but at its best this category captures how humans actually reason through problems collaboratively and in real time.
bullets: Reddit: 800M+ posts and comments across 130,000+ active subreddits
StackExchange: 200+ Q&A sites beyond Stack Overflow, covering every domain
The 'explain like I'm five' register — lay explanations of complex topics — comes from here

cells.label: Reddit (curated)
cells.description: Filtered Reddit, keeping high-karma, high-engagement posts from substantive subreddits. The raw Reddit corpus is huge and low-quality; curated versions (Pushshift + quality filter) keep the 'explain it to me' discussions, in-depth analyses, and expert AMAs.
bullets: r/explainlikeimfive: lay explanations of complex topics — teaches the skill of analogy
r/AskScience, r/AskHistorians: expert-verified answers, a rare quality signal in forum data
Pushshift Reddit dataset: ~600GB of raw posts; most pipelines filter to <10% of that
cells.screenshotCaption: r/explainlikeimfive — the 'explain it simply' register that models learned to mimic

cells.label: StackExchange / Forums
cells.description: The full StackExchange network beyond Stack Overflow: Math, Physics, English Language & Usage, Cooking, Travel, Law, and 200+ other communities. Each site has its own conventions for what a good answer looks like.
bullets: StackExchange data dump: all 200+ sites, fully public under CC-BY-SA
Math.SE, Physics.SE: semi-formal reasoning in natural language + LaTeX
English Language & Usage, Writing: teaches models about language itself
cells.screenshotCaption: Stack Overflow — voted answers create a rare quality signal in forum data
